figures.caption: Figure 4: Computational scaling with state dimension n: (a) wall-clock training time for a 200-epoch warmup, (b) parameter count fitted as O(n1.37), (c) relative growth of DFPS parameters vs. theoretical FDM grid size Gn (G = 20). FDM does not apply to the random-coefficient setting and is shown only for reference.
Table 2: Distributions and dimensions of the randomized state and cost coefficients.

Figure 7: Mean-variance portfolio Stackelberg game (κ = 0.50, γ = 0.15): (a) stock-holding deviation X1(t) over simulated paths (mean ±1σ), (b) investor’s mean optimal trading rate E[u1(t)], (c) equilibrium costs J1 and J2 under deterministic vs. stochastic volatility (medium-vol regime, σ ∈ [0.10, 0.20]).
Table 6: Equilibrium costs under varying volatility regimes (mean ± Monte Carlo standard deviation over 5 independent path realizations; coefficient instances are fixed per regime).

Figure 1: Training convergence of the DFPS algorithm: (a) follower cost J1 across Picard iterations (dashed line: last-10 mean = 0.354, shaded band: ±1 std), (b) follower BSDE residual, (c) mean-field constraint violations Vu,1 and Vx,1 with tolerance εtol = 0.02, (d) leader BSDE residual across Picard iterations.
Figure 6: Stackelberg vs. Nash-type baseline across 8 financial scenarios: (a) leader cost per scenario, (b) perscenario advantage ∆J2 = JNash
Table 1: Network architectures and initialization parameters. All hidden layers use Tanh activation functions.

Figure 5: Unilateral deviation test: cost increment ∆Ji(ε) vs. perturbation magnitude ε, averaged over 6 seeds with a shaded ±1σ band. Grey region: ±1% numerical tolerance. Both curves attain their minimum near ε = 0 and remain within tolerance, providing empirical evidence of local Stackelberg optimality.
Table 5: Ablation study (mean ± std. over 5 independent runs, N = 100, M = 64). Positive ∆Ji indicates cost degradation relative to Full DFPS.

Figure 2: Adaptive ALM diagnostics: constraint violations (left axes, log scale) and penalty parameters ρ (right axes, dotted) over accumulated inner optimization steps. Dashed horizontal line: tolerance εtol = 0.02. All four violations terminate below tolerance, consistent with Proposition 4.3.

Figure 3: Temporal discretization convergence (constant-coefficient setting): (a) follower cost JN 1 vs. Riccati reference J∗ 1 = 0.225, (b) BSDE residual, (c) self-convergence error on log-log scale with fitted slope 1.30.
